source_record.title: Single-cell profiling in multiple myeloma: insights, problems, and promises.
source_record.study_context: Blood review discussing single-cell profiling in multiple myeloma, including research insights, technical problems, and translational promises. This public extraction uses the review only for implementation-readiness and translation-gap context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source frames single-cell profiling as a powerful research approach in multiple myeloma while explicitly foregrounding problems and translational limitations; this supports keeping the single-cell translation gap as a research-tooling and implementation-readiness layer separate from biological mechanism claims.
limitations: Review-level source; not a clinical implementation study.
Does not establish that single-cell profiling should be ordered for any individual.
Does not validate a specific single-cell platform, bioinformatics pipeline, monitoring workflow, or treatment decision.
Supports translation-gap navigation only, not clinical readiness or patient-specific interpretation.

what_this_supports: The single-cell-translation-gap-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has two review-level sources that preserve implementation barriers.
The geometry should keep technology-readiness context separate from biological mechanism nodes.
Future single-cell evidence should record platform, data-processing, workflow, and clinical-translation constraints before comparison.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not recommend single-cell profiling, sequencing, or any test platform for a person.
It does not make single-cell results clinically actionable.
It does not validate treatment selection, monitoring, prognosis, trial fit, or target selection.
It does not validate a cure claim.

review_notes: Created for Phase 6 falsification and hypothesis generation.
Source details checked against PubMed metadata for PMID 37196627.
The extraction intentionally treats single-cell translation as tooling readiness, not clinical implementation.
